name: org.machanism.machai:assembly-maven-plugin
description: assembly-maven-plugin is a Maven plugin (packaging: maven-plugin) in the Machanism MachAI ecosystem that automates project "assembly" by combining GenAI-assisted library recommendation with an automated application assembly workflow driven by bindex metadata. It provides the Maven goal "assembly" (requiresProject=false) so it can be executed outside a standard Maven project and applies changes to a configured base directory (${basedir}). The workflow: (1) obtains a free-form assembly prompt either from a configured prompt file (default project.txt, property assembly.prompt.file) or interactively via Plexus Prompter; (2) loads configuration from bindex.properties via PropertiesConfigurator; (3) runs org.machanism.machai.bindex.Picker using a configurable GenAI provider/model (property Picker.MODEL_PROP_NAME, default Picker.DEFAULT_MODEL) and an optional bindex registration/lookup URL (property bindex.register.url) to recommend relevant libraries as org.machanism.machai.schema.Bindex entries, filtering by a minimum score threshold (property assembly.score, default ApplicationAssembly.DEFAULT_SCORE_VALUE) and logging recommended library ids and scores; (4) runs org.machanism.machai.bindex.ApplicationAssembly using a configurable GenAI provider/model (property ApplicationAssembly.MODEL_PROP_NAME, default ApplicationAssembly.DEFAULT_MODEL) to apply changes to the project directory, optionally logging inputs controlled by ApplicationAssembly.LOG_INPUTS_PROP_NAME. Primary use cases: generating or modifying a project structure and its dependencies based on a natural-language prompt; recommending libraries based on semantic search over bindex metadata; automating integration steps in a Maven-managed directory. It depends on MachAI bindex-core and Maven/Plexus APIs and uses Apache Commons IO for prompt file reading. Typical invocation is via Maven plugin execution, supplying an assembly prompt file or using interactive prompting, then allowing the plugin to recommend and integrate libraries into the target directory.

constructors.description: Factory method used by the Mojo to create a Picker instance configured with the picker model and optional bindex registration URL. Exists primarily to allow overriding picker creation in tests.

examples.description: Creates a Picker using a PropertiesConfigurator-backed configuration source.

constructors.description: Factory method used by the Mojo to create an ApplicationAssembly instance configured with the assembly model, configuration source, and the Maven execution base directory (basedir). Exists primarily to allow overriding assembly creation in tests.

examples.description: Creates an ApplicationAssembly and sets the project directory before running the assembly workflow.

features.name: execute
features.description: Executes the Maven goal "assembly". Reads the assembly prompt from a configured file (default project.txt) if it exists, otherwise prompts the user interactively. Loads configuration from bindex.properties, runs the MachAI Picker to recommend libraries (Bindex entries) above a configured score threshold, logs recommended libraries and their scores, then runs the MachAI ApplicationAssembly workflow to apply changes to the configured project base directory. Wraps I/O and prompting failures in MojoExecutionException.

examples.description: Illustrates programmatic execution of the Mojo (typically Maven invokes this automatically during a build or direct goal execution).

examples.name: Maven goal usage
examples.description: Run the assembly workflow against a directory using a prompt file (default project.txt) or interactively if the file is missing.

examples.name: Prompt source behavior
examples.description: If the prompt file does not exist, the plugin asks for a free-form prompt via Plexus Prompter and then proceeds with picker recommendations and application assembly.
